image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Planets don't orbit in perfect circles — they dance in ellipses.
Context visual: In the early 1600s, Johannes Kepler cracked the code of planetary movement.
Escalation visual: He discovered that planets sweep out equal areas in equal times.
Evidence visual: Imagine a line from the sun to a planet. It covers the same area in a given time, whether it’s close or far.
Payoff visual: This means planets move faster when they're nearer to the sun — gravity really is a game changer!
Cta visual: Curious about more cosmic mysteries? Like and follow for more!
